Command.Name: npm
Command.Description: Manage and evaluate npm package versions

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: (Optional) [Gray]The path to the package.json file or the folder it resides. If not specified, the current working directory will be used.[/]

Option.Description: [Gray]Only to be used during [Blue]development[/] to include additional columns in the list for debugging purposes.[/]
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. "package1,package2,package3".[/]

[Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]npm install[/] command even if you don't provide the [Blue]--update[/] argument. This is useful when checking for vulnerabilities when the package-lock.json still doesn't exist.[/]
[Gray]Find the latest version of the specified major number.[/]
[Gray]Only evaluate the lastest version of the current major.[/]
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. "package1,package2,package3".[/]
[Gray]Include prerelease versions when evaluating the latest version. (Alpha, Beta, Release Candidate, Nightly Builds, etc).[/]
[Gray]Include package project url in the list.[/]
[Gray]When using the [Blue]--update[/] argument, we won't ask for confirmation to continue or to install the packages. Both operations will be executed.[/]

[Gray]Update the packages to desired version. Latest version by default, lastest of the current Major when the [blue]--minor[/] option is specified or latest requested version when the [blue]--major <n>[/] option is specified.[/]
[Gray]Include package URL in the list.[/]
[Gray]Include package version url in the list.[/]

Command.Name: nuget
Command.Description: Manage and evaluate NuGet package versions

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: (Optional) [Gray]The path to the csproj or packages.props file or the solution folder it resides. If not specified, the current working directory will be used.[/]

Option.Description: [Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]build[/] command even if you don't provide the [Blue]--update[/] argument. This is useful to check if your code is compatible with the installed packages.[/]
[Gray]Only to be used during [Blue]development[/] to include additional columns in the list for debugging purposes.[/]
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. "package1,package2,package3".[/]

[Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]restore[/] command even if you don't provide the [Blue]--update[/] argument. This is useful when checking for vulnerabilities when the packages haven't being installed yet.[/]
[Gray]Find the latest version of the specified major number.[/]
[Gray]Only evaluate the lastest version of the current major.[/]
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names to filter the packages during analysis. Ex. "package1,package2,package3".[/]
[Gray]Include prerelease versions when evaluating the latest version. (Alpha, Beta, Release Candidate, Nightly Builds, etc).[/]
[Gray]Include package project url in the list.[/]
[Gray]Run the [DarkOrange]restore[/] command before evaluating packages. This is useful to install the updated packages right away.[/]
[Gray]When using the [Blue]--update[/] argument, we won't ask for confirmation to continue or to install the packages. Both operations will be executed.[/]

[Gray]Update the packages to desired version. Latest version by default, lastest of the current Major when the [blue]--minor[/] option is specified or latest requested version when the [blue]--major <n>[/] option is specified.[/]
[Gray]Include package URL in the list.[/]
[Gray]Include package version url in the list.[/]

Command.Name: dotnet
Command.Description: Manage and evaluate Dotnet Framework versions

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: (Optional) [Gray]The path to the csproj or the solution folder it resides. If not specified, the current working directory will be used.[/]

Option.Description: [Gray]Only to be used during [Blue]development[/] to include additional columns in the list for debugging purposes.[/]
[Gray]Use this option to install a new version of the [green]dotnet[/] Framework. If no version is defined, you will be able to selection from a list.[/]
[Gray]A comma-separated list of names or partial names to filter the projects during analysis. Ex. "project1,project2,project3".[/]
[Gray]List the available [pink1]dotnet Runtime Releases[/] and compare with the installed versions.[/]
[Gray]List the available [pink1]dotnet SDK Releases[/] and compare with the installed versions.[/]
[Gray]We won't ask for confirmation to continue, update or install. All operations will be executed.[/]
[Gray]Update the [green]dotnet[/] version of the project(s). If you define [blue]latest[/], the latest version will be used. If no version is defined, you will be able to selection from a list.[/]
